title: erysimum asperum subsp. capitatum
intro: Erysimum asperum subsp. capitatum, also known as Western Wallflower, is a biennial or perennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its distinctive yellow or orange flowers. The medicinal properties of Erysimum asperum subsp. capitatum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as arthritis and gastrointestinal problems. Erysimum asperum subsp. capitatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
